title: Forcepoint Web Security On-Premises URL Blocks (`websense:cg:kv`)
dataSources: Forcepoint Web Security / Websense proxy syslog key-value logs, `sourcetype=websense:cg:kv`
app: Splunk Add-on for Forcepoint Web Security (Splunkbase 2966)
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="websense:cg:kv" earliest=-7d
| eval act=lower(coalesce(action, disposition, result))
| where match(act,"(?i)block|deny|drop")
| stats count by user, url, category, policy
| sort -count
| head 40
description: Traditional Forcepoint Web Security appliances still enforce acceptable-use and threat categories for many enterprises. Blocked URL telemetry validates policy strictness, identifies compromised accounts generating unusual categories, and supports HR or insider investigations.
value: Web security teams analyze Forcepoint on-premises proxy URL blocks by category and user, distinguishing security-category blocks indicating endpoint compromise from acceptable-use policy enforcement, with migration guidance for Forcepoint ONE SSE.
implementation: Forward appliance syslog to Splunk with the vendor-recommended KV format. Install Splunkbase 2966 for CIM Web mappings. Map `category` to internal risk tiers. Deprecation planning: migrate searches to Forcepoint ONE (`forcepoint:one`) as appliances are replaced.
visualization: Table (user, URL category), Bar chart (blocks by category), Timeline.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count

references.title: Splunk Documentation — Source types for Splunk Add-on for Forcepoint Web Security
Splunkbase — Splunk Add-on for Forcepoint Web Security

knownFalsePositives: Access denied for users in mid-onboarding, role changes pending propagation, or VDI sessions with new device IDs.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Forcepoint Web Security (formerly Websense) proxy logs in `index=proxy` with `sourcetype=websense:cg:kv`. Key fields: `user` (authenticated user), `url` (requested URL), `category` (URL category), `action` (permitted/blocked), `policy` (policy name), `src_ip`, `bytes`, `status_code`, `content_type`.
• Forcepoint Web Security uses URL categorization (100+ categories), reputation, and threat intelligence to filter web traffic. The `websense:cg:kv` sourcetype is the SIEM key-value export format from the Content Gateway proxy.
• Install Splunkbase 2966 (Splunk Add-on for Forcepoint Web Security) for CIM Web mappings.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
On Forcepoint Content Gateway: Settings > Log Server > SIEM Integration > add Splunk syslog.

Verify:
```spl
index=proxy sourcetype="websense:cg:kv" earliest=-24h
| stats count by action
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — URL block analysis:
```spl
index=proxy sourcetype="websense:cg:kv" action=blocked earliest=-7d
| timechart span=1d count by category limit=10
```

Understanding this SPL: Trends blocked URL categories over time. Security categories (Malware, Phishing, Botnets) indicate active threats. Policy categories (Adult, Gambling, Streaming) indicate acceptable-use enforcement. A spike in security blocks from specific users may indicate endpoint compromise.

User investigation:
```spl
index=proxy sourcetype="websense:cg:kv" action=blocked earliest=-24h
| where match(category, "(?i)malware|phishing|botnet|command|spyware")
| stats count dc(url) as unique_urls by user, src_ip, category
| sort - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Visit a blocked-category URL. Verify block event.
(b) In Forcepoint Security Manager: Reporting > Web Activity.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Block category trend. Row 2 — Security category users. Row 3 — Top blocked URLs.
Alert: When user generates 10+ security-category blocks in 1 hour (possible compromise).

Runbook:
1. **Security category blocks** — Endpoint compromised? Check for malware.
2. **Policy category spike** — Awareness training needed.
3. **Uncategorized blocks** — Submit URLs for re-categorization.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No proxy events** — SIEM integration may not be enabled on Content Gateway.
• **`category` shows numbers** — Install Splunkbase 2966 for category name lookups.

Migration note: As Forcepoint Web Security appliances are replaced by Forcepoint ONE SSE, migrate these searches to `sourcetype=forcepoint:one:sse`.
grandmaExplanation: Our on-premises web filter blocks dangerous and inappropriate websites. We watch what it blocks — when someone's computer keeps trying to visit malware sites, it probably means their computer is infected and we need to clean it up.
